---
title: "M09-2-Application Assignment: Data Import and Export"
author: "Raymond Marquez"
date: '`r format(Sys.time())`'
format: 
  html: 
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    number-sections: true
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-fold: false
    code-link: true
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
execute: 
  freeze: auto
warning: false
error: true
---

# Prompt 1 

```{r}
# Load required packages
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Q1.1 
raw_items <- read_csv(
  "data/items_ordered.csv",
  skip = 1,
  col_names = FALSE,
  col_types = cols(.default = "c")
)

items <- raw_items %>%
  separate(X1, into = c("customer_id","order_date","item","quantity","price"), sep = ";") %>%
  mutate(
    quantity = as.numeric(quantity),
    price = as.numeric(price)
  )

glimpse(items)

# Q1.2
items %>%
  filter(price == max(price, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  select(item)
```

# Prompt 2

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)

# --- Q2.1: Read Excel file ---
items_excel <- read_excel("data/items_ordered.xlsx")
glimpse(items_excel)

# --- Q2.2: Plot and export ---
dir.create("images", showWarnings = FALSE)

plot_obj <- ggplot(items_excel, aes(item, quantity)) +
  geom_col() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Item Quantities")

ggsave("images/items.png", plot_obj)
ggsave("images/items.pdf", plot_obj)
ggsave("images/items.jpg", plot_obj)
```

# Prompt 3

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(naniar)
library(writexl)

# --- Q3.1: Import clipboard data ---
exec_data <- read.delim("clipboard", header = TRUE)

exec_clean <- exec_data %>%
  replace_with_na_all(~ . %in% c("N/A", "--"))

glimpse(exec_clean)

# --- Q3.2: Export cleaned data ---
write.csv(exec_clean, "data/dis_exec_clean.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write_xlsx(exec_clean, "data/dis_exec_clean.xlsx")
```
# Prompt 4

```{r}
library(rvest)
library(stringr)

# Load webpage
url <- "https://web.archive.org/web/20220201012049/https://www.imdb.com/chart/top/"
page <- read_html(url)

# Q4.1: Extract titles
titles <- page %>%
  html_elements(".titleColumn a") %>%
  html_text(trim = TRUE)

# Q4.2: Extract years 
years <- page %>%
  html_elements(".secondaryInfo") %>%
  html_text() %>%
  str_remove_all("[()]")

# Q4.3: Extract ratings 
ratings <- page %>%
  html_elements(".imdbRating strong") %>%
  html_text() %>%
  as.numeric()

head(titles)
head(years)
head(ratings)
```
# Prompt 5

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# Combine scraped data
movies <- tibble(
  title = titles,
  year = years,
  rating = ratings
) %>%
  arrange(desc(rating))

head(movies)
tail(movies)
```
# Prompt 6

```{r}
library(naniar)
library(tibble)

# Create messy dataset
sample_data <- tribble(
  ~name, ~x, ~y, ~z,
  "N/A",1,"N/A",-100,
  "N A",3,"NOt available",-99,
  "N / A",NA,"29",-98,
  "Not Available",-99,"25",-101,
  "John Smith",-98,"28",-1
)

# Q6.1 
sample_data %>% replace_with_na_all(~ . == "N/A")

# Q6.2 
sample_data %>% replace_with_na_all(~ . %in% c("N/A","N A","N / A"))

# Q6.3 
sample_data %>% replace_with_na_all(~ . %in% c(-99,-98))

# Q6.4 
# replace_with_na_all() is efficient for handling multiple missing patterns at once
```
# Prompt 7

```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)
library(WDI)
library(ggplot2)

# Q7.1: Load and filter
ratio_data <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/master/data/2019/2019-05-07/student_teacher_ratio.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

filtered <- ratio_data %>%
  filter(year == 2016,
         indicator == "Secondary Education",
         !is.na(student_ratio))

# Q7.2: World Bank data
wb <- WDI(
  country = "all",
  indicator = c("NY.GDP.PCAP.CD","SP.POP.TOTL"),
  start = 2016,
  end = 2016,
  extra = TRUE
)

# Q7.3: Merge and histogram 
merged <- filtered %>%
  left_join(wb, by = c("country_code" = "iso3c"))

ggplot(merged, aes(student_ratio)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20)

# Q7.4: Scatter plot 
ggplot(merged, aes(NY.GDP.PCAP.CD, student_ratio, size = SP.POP.TOTL)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = region), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)

# Q7.5: Facet plot
ggplot(merged, aes(NY.GDP.PCAP.CD, student_ratio)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~region) +
  scale_x_log10()
```
# Prompt 8

```{r}
library(haven)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Q8.1: Load SPSS data 
demo <- read_sav("data/demo.sav")

# Q8.2: Frequency tables 
table(demo$employ)
table(demo$retire)
table(demo$empcat)
table(demo$jobsat)

# Q8.3: Visualization 
demo %>%
  select(wireless:callwait) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything()) %>%
  ggplot(aes(name, fill = factor(value))) +
  geom_bar()
```
# Prompt 9

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Q9.1: Create score
demo <- demo %>%
  mutate(tech_score = rowSums(select(., wireless:callwait), na.rm = TRUE))

# Q9.2: Group comparison 
demo %>%
  group_by(retire) %>%
  summarise(avg_score = mean(tech_score, na.rm = TRUE))

# Q9.3
# Retired individuals generally show lower engagement with technology-related tools
```
# Prompt 10

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

tech_long <- demo %>%
  select(wireless:callwait) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything())

summary_stats <- tech_long %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarise(
    proportion = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE)/sqrt(n())
  )

ggplot(summary_stats, aes(reorder(name, proportion), proportion)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = proportion - 1.96*se,
                    ymax = proportion + 1.96*se)) +
  coord_flip()
```
